heading: Identification, review, and implementation of proposals and regional projects
label: 941c Identification, review, and implementation of proposals and regional projects

children.text: shall encourage the development and, subject to the availability of appropriations, the implementation of fish and wildlife restoration proposals and regional projects based on the results of the Report; and

in cooperation with the State Directors and Indian Tribes, shall identify, develop, and, subject to the availability of appropriations, implement regional projects in the Great Lakes Basin to be administered by Director in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Identification of proposals and regional projects
children.label: b Identification of proposals and regional projects

children.heading: Request by the Director
children.label: 1 Request by the Director
children.text: The Director shall annually request that State Directors and Indian Tribes, in cooperation or partnership with other interested entities and in accordance with subsection (a), submit proposals or regional projects for the restoration of fish and wildlife resources.

children.heading: Requirements for proposals and regional projects
children.label: 2 Requirements for proposals and regional projects
A
children.text: submitted in the manner and form prescribed by the Director; and

children.label: i
children.text: the goals of the Great Lakes Water Quality Agreement, as amended;

children.label: ii
children.text: the 1954 Great Lakes Fisheries Convention;

children.label: iii
children.text: the 1980 Joint Strategic Plan for Management of Great Lakes Fisheries, as revised in 1997, and Fish Community Objectives for each Great Lake and connecting water as established under the Joint Strategic Plan;

children.label: iv
children.text: the Nonindigenous Aquatic Nuisance Prevention and Control Act of 1990 ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 4701

children.label: v
children.text: the North American Waterfowl Management Plan and joint ventures established under the plan;

children.label: vi
children.text: the strategies outlined through the Great Lakes Regional Collaboration authorized under Executive Order 13340 (69 Fed. Reg. 29043; relating to the Great Lakes Interagency Task Force);

children.label: vii
children.text: the strategic action plan of the Great Lakes Restoration Initiative; and

children.label: viii
children.text: each applicable State wildlife action plan.

children.heading: Sea lamprey authority
children.label: 3 Sea lamprey authority
children.text: The Great Lakes Fishery Commission shall retain authority and responsibility to formulate and implement a comprehensive program to eradicate or minimize sea lamprey populations in the Great Lakes Basin.

children.heading: Establishment of Committee
children.label: 1 Establishment of Committee
children.text: There is established the Great Lakes Fish and Wildlife Restoration Proposal Review Committee, which shall operate under the guidance of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service.

children.label: i
children.text: 1 representative shall be the individual appointed by the State Director or Indian Tribe to the Council of Lake Committees of the Great Lakes Fishery Commission; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 representative shall have expertise in wildlife management.

children.heading: Appointments
children.label: B Appointments
children.text: Each representative shall serve at the pleasure of the appointing State Director or Tribal Chair.

children.heading: Observer
children.label: C Observer
children.text: The United States Fish and Wildlife Service shall participate as an observer of the Committee.

children.heading: Recusal
children.label: D Recusal
children.text: A member of the Committee shall recuse himself or herself from consideration of proposals that the member, or the entity that the member represents, has submitted.

children.label: A
children.text: meet at least annually;

children.label: B
children.text: review proposals and regional projects developed in accordance with subsection (b) to assess the effectiveness and appropriateness of the proposals and regional projects in fulfilling the purposes of this chapter; and

children.label: C
children.text: recommend to the Director any of those proposals and regional projects that should be funded and implemented under this section.

children.heading: Implementation of proposals and regional projects
children.label: d Implementation of proposals and regional projects
A
children.text: select proposals and regional projects to be implemented; and

children.label: B
children.text: subject to the availability of appropriations and subsection (e), fund implementation of the proposals and regional projects.

children.heading: Selection criteria
children.label: 2 Selection criteria
children.text: In selecting and funding proposals and regional projects, the Director shall take into account the effectiveness and appropriateness of the proposals and regional projects in fulfilling the purposes of other laws applicable to restoration of the fish and wildlife resources and habitat of the Great Lakes Basin.

children.heading: Non-Federal share
children.label: A Non-Federal share
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (3) and (5) and subject to paragraph (2), not less than 25 percent of the cost of implementing a proposal or regional project selected under subsection (d) (excluding the cost of establishing sea lamprey barriers) shall be paid in cash or in-kind contributions by non-Federal sources.

children.heading: Time period for providing match
children.label: B Time period for providing match
children.text: The non-Federal share of the cost of implementing a proposal or regional project required under subparagraph (A) may be provided at any time during the 2-year period preceding January 1 of the year in which the Director receives the application for the proposal or regional project.

children.label: i
children.text: the appraised value of land or a conservation easement as described in subparagraph (B); or

children.label: I
children.text: securing a conservation easement; and

children.label: II
children.text: restoration or enhancement of the conservation easement.

children.label: I
children.text: meets the requirements of subsection (b)(2);

children.label: II
children.text: is acquired before the end of the grant period of the proposal or regional project;

children.label: III
children.text: is held in perpetuity for the conservation purposes of the programs of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service related to the Great Lakes Basin, as described in , by an accredited land trust or conservancy or a Federal, State, or tribal agency;section 941d of this title

children.label: IV
children.text: is connected either physically or through a conservation planning process to the proposal or regional project; and

children.label: V
children.text: is appraised in accordance with clause (ii).

children.label: I
children.text: the appraisal valuation date shall be not later than 1 year after the price of the conservation easement was set under a contract; and

children.label: aa
children.text: conform to the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP); and

children.label: bb
children.text: be completed by a Federal- or State-certified appraiser.

children.heading: Costs of securing conservation easements
children.label: C Costs of securing conservation easements

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: All costs associated with securing a conservation easement and restoration or enhancement of that conservation easement may be used to satisfy the non-Federal share of the cost of implementing a proposal or regional project required under paragraph (1)(A) if the activities and expenses associated with securing the conservation easement and restoration or enhancement of that conservation easement meet the requirements of subparagraph (B)(i).

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: ii Inclusion
children.text: The costs referred to in clause (i) may include cash, in-kind contributions, and indirect costs.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: iii Exclusion
children.text: The costs referred to in clause (i) may not be costs associated with mitigation or litigation (other than costs associated with the Natural Resource Damage Assessment program).

children.heading: Regional projects
children.label: 3 Regional projects
children.text: Regional projects selected under subsection (d) shall be exempt from cost sharing if the Director determines that the authorization for the project does not require a non-Federal cost-share.

children.heading: Exclusion of Federal funds from non-Federal share
children.label: 4 Exclusion of Federal funds from non-Federal share
children.text: The Director may not consider the expenditure, directly or indirectly, of Federal funds received by any entity to be a contribution by a non-Federal source for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Effect on certain Indian tribes
children.label: 5 Effect on certain Indian tribes
children.text: Nothing in this subsection affects an Indian tribe affected by an alternative applicable cost sharing requirement under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 4501See References in Text note below.1
